title: Production step-merging decisions match rendered English description text ("Subtract", "-(", "Move terms to one side", "1^", "-> 1")
evidence: step_cleanup.rs:244-252 (production, not test): merges consecutive steps when prev_desc.contains("Subtract") && curr_desc.contains("Subtract") && (curr_desc.contains("-(") || contains("- -(")) or curr_desc == "Move terms to one side". step_optimization_runtime.rs:64-67: drops a step when rule_name == "Evaluate Numeric Power" && description.contains("1^") && contains("-> 1") — matching formatter output embedded in the description.
why: These matchers silently stop firing when step phrasing or formatter rendering changes — and the repo just ran a whole i18n campaign (cycle D) localizing solve-step descriptions at the wire boundary; if description generation is ever localized earlier in the pipeline (the natural next step of that campaign), these merge/drop heuristics quietly degrade the didactic output with no test signal except downstream snapshot churn.
fix: Bounded: where these steps are created, attach a structured tag (e.g. StepMeta field or StepCategory variant like SubtractBothSides / TrivialPowerOfOne) and match the tag instead of the text. Producer sites are findable by grepping the matched literals; huella-safe because behavior is preserved for identical inputs.

title: Variable identity is stringly-typed end to end: var: &str threaded 414 times through 81 files and compared via ctx.sym_name(sym) == var per AST node
evidence: grep count: 414 occurrences of `var: &str` across 81 files in the crate; identity checks like substitution.rs:1260 `Expr::Variable(sym_id) => ctx.sym_name(*sym_id) == var` and :1352, domain_condition.rs resolves sym_name 13 times; every layer of the runtime tower forwards `var: &str` and a separate FSymName callback (e.g. solve_runtime_flow_isolation_default_routes.rs:83) even though SymbolId exists in cas_ast.
why: It bakes a string-comparison into the hottest traversals (contains_var runs per isolation step per node) and makes multi-variable/system work — a stated campaign direction — clumsier: there is no typed place to hang variable metadata, and any future scoping/shadowing semantics would have to be retrofitted through 81 files.
fix: Do NOT thread a new type through the tower now (that is the shotgun surgery this report warns about). Bounded first step: intern once at the solve entrypoints (solve_entry.rs / solver_entrypoints_bound_runtime.rs) to a SymbolId and add SymbolId-based twins of the 3 hot helpers (isolation_utils::contains_var, substitution internals), converting call sites opportunistically as files are touched. Full migration only after the runtime tower collapses (finding 1), which removes most of the threading cost.

title: Flat 181-file namespace encodes the missing module hierarchy in 60-90 char file names, hiding the architecture the file set actually has
evidence: All 181 modules sit flat in src/ with hierarchy embedded in names: solve_runtime_pipeline_strategy_execute_apply_entrypoint_context_runtime.rs (73 chars), solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_reentrant_context_runtime.rs, dispatch_isolation_with_default_isolated_and_negated_entries_and_default_linear_collect_kernels_for_var_and_unified_step_mapper_with_state (135-char fn name, solve_runtime_flow_isolation_dispatch_base.rs:7). The real layering (bound_runtime -> context_runtime -> runtime -> flow -> kernels) is only recoverable by reading suffix conventions; tiny facade files exist solely to simulate directories (solve_runtime_flow.rs = 12 lines of pub use).
why: Minor on its own but it compounds findings 1 and 5: reviewers and scout agents cannot see which of the 61 runtime files are facade vs plumbing vs kernel, which is part of why dead 15-generic functions and stale facade docs (solve_runtime_flow.rs:3 claims downstream imports that do not exist) went unnoticed.
fix: Fold into the finding-1 collapse rather than a standalone rename storm: as each layer is merged, place survivors under directory modules (solve_runtime/{bound,context,flow}/...) so remaining names shrink naturally. Zero-cost piece doable now: fix the stale doc comments on the facade modules.

title: Per-integration-form derivative 'presentation twins' spread each form family across two support god files with only naming as the link
evidence: symbolic_differentiation_support.rs (8,576 lines, 210 fns, tests from 6,892) contains form-specific pub twins of integration primitives: differentiate_hyperbolic_cosh_fourth_tanh_cubic_primitive (5468), differentiate_hyperbolic_sinh_fourth_reciprocal_tanh_cubic_primitive (5630), positive_constant_radius_quadratic_log_arctan_primitive_derivative (1565), rational_*_log_abs_pole_derivative (667/755/833) — each consumed by exactly one cas_engine presentation rule (per-fn usage scan: 1-2 external refs each, e.g. rules/calculus/tanh_primitive_derivative_presentation.rs). The main dispatcher differentiate_symbolic_expr (6392, ~500 lines, 72 return arms) is separate from these twins.
why: Changing an integration form's output shape requires finding and syncing its derivative twin by name convention only; a missed twin degrades presentation/verification silently. Lower severity than the integration contract because volume is smaller (~10 pub twins).
fix: When a form family gets its per-family module (see the descriptor finding), move its derivative twin into the same module so the pair is colocated; no standalone campaign needed.

title: compute_poly_gcd_unified_with: 10 params with 2 injected callbacks, returning (ExprId, String) human-readable description from the math layer
evidence: Signature read at 162-176: (ctx, a, b, goal, mode, modp_preset, modp_main_var, pre_evaluate: FPreEval, render: FRender) -> (ExprId, String) with #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]; desc strings built inline (:210 format!("poly_gcd({}, {})", render(ctx,a), render(ctx,b)), :231 "poly_gcd(..., modp) [blocked for soundness]"). 2 external callers (cas_engine rules/number_theory.rs:46, rules/algebra/poly_gcd.rs:34) — the render callback exists solely because presentation lives below the formatter.
why: Presentation strings born in cas_math cannot be localized or restyled without touching the math crate (same hazard the solve-description i18n cycle had to patch at the wire boundary), and the callback pair is the crate's widest signature (max in the 7+-param scan).
fix: Return a small enum/struct (GcdPath { mode, layer_used, blocked_reason }) and let the two cas_engine callers format the description; drop the render callback. Bounded: 3 files.

title: pattern_scanner matches builtin functions by string name while 563 sites crate-wide use the BuiltinFn enum
evidence: Production sites (tests begin at :654): :226-230 match ctx.sym_name(*fn_id) { "sin" => ..., "cos" => ... }, :440 if name == "tan" && args.len() == 1, :277-287 inverse-pair matching by name string. BuiltinFn::Tan exists (cas_ast/src/builtin.rs:58) and the crate otherwise uses is_builtin/builtin_of (563 grep hits). A user-defined symbol named 'tan' would satisfy sym_name=="tan" but not is_builtin — the two predicates disagree on shadowed names.
why: Fragile secondary matching path: identity rules fired by pattern_scanner can treat a non-builtin symbol as the trig function, and the inconsistency invites copy-paste into new scanners. Minor because the scanner is only a hint layer; would confirm impact by testing a user function named tan through the sin^2+cos^2 and tan-triple scanners.
fix: Replace the ~4 sym_name string comparisons in pattern_scanner.rs with ctx.is_builtin(fn_id, BuiltinFn::Sin/Cos/Tan) to match the crate convention.

title: Summary of remaining minor observations and verified-clean areas
evidence: Clean: cas_math's Cargo.toml depends only on cas_ast (no upward layering); prove_sign->root_forms/const_sign consultation order (prove_sign.rs:296-312) is sound layering, no duplicated sign logic found; general_integration_backend keeps tests in a separate tests.rs (6,166 lines) unlike the god files; summation_support.rs (4,758 lines, 27 pub fns) is cohesive around finite sum/product planning; the ~150 small fraction_*/trig_* support modules average <1k lines and show the extract-before-abstract strategy working. Minor: four near-duplicate linear-coefficient extractors with different shapes (symbolic_integration_support.rs:23529 get_linear_coeffs, trig_linear_support.rs:23 extract_linear_coefficients, summation_support.rs:1554 extract_linear_offset, multinomial_expand.rs:225 extract_linear_terms) — unify only opportunistically; gcd_zippel_modp.rs has three 9-param sample-collector fns (409/507/577) that would take a SampleConfig struct; internal-only pub fns also found in symbolic_integration_support (4: e.g. is_sec_third_affine_target, is_csc_third_affine_target at 5895/5913).
why: Records what was checked and found healthy so the campaign does not re-audit it, plus small items not worth individual findings.
fix: No action for the clean areas; fold the minor items into adjacent cycles (SampleConfig struct when touching zippel; demote the 4 internal-only predicates with the visibility sweep).

title: Two one-shot Rust codemod scripts (669 lines) remain executable long after their migration completed
evidence: inject_assumption_events.py (338 lines) and remove_domain_assumption.py (331 lines) are brace-counting regex codemods over crates/**/*.rs with --apply modes. Referenced only by docs/ASSUMPTIONS_POLICY.md and themselves (repo-wide grep); not wired into Makefile/ci.sh/tests. The fields they migrate (assumption_events, domain_assumption) still exist in today's crates (cas_engine/src/rule.rs, cas_solver_core), so a stale re-run with --apply would regex-edit current source under drifted struct shapes.
why: Dead tooling that can mutate the engine's source is worse than ordinary dead code; it also inflates the scripts/ surface this harness audit has to reason about.
fix: Delete both (git history preserves them) or move to docs/archive/ with the .py extension dropped; update the two references in docs/ASSUMPTIONS_POLICY.md to point at the commit hash instead.

title: Bench targets are ~60 hand-expanded base/-save/-compare triplets (~450 of 715 lines) instead of pattern rules
evidence: 99 `cargo bench` recipe lines; 56 `-save:`/`-compare:` stanzas; representative triplet bench-engine-fast/-save/-compare @268-281 differs only in the trailing criterion flag (`--save-baseline` vs `--baseline`) and the BASELINE guard; the giant single-line .PHONY list @1 must be edited in lockstep for every addition. Some consolidation exists (bench-engine-solve-hotspots-* delegate to -seq targets @300-304), proving the seam. test_engine_makefile_targets.py pins only the engine-scorecard targets, not bench stanzas, so a collapse is test-safe.
why: Pure maintenance tax: every new bench = 3 near-identical stanzas + a .PHONY edit; inconsistencies (a -save without env prefix) would silently benchmark a different configuration.
fix: Collapse each family with a canned recipe/define parameterized on env prefix + package + bench name, or static pattern rules (bench-%-save/bench-%-compare) reading a per-family variable table; keep target names identical so muscle memory and docs survive.

title: Summary of verified minor items: text-format contracts with Rust harness stdout, colab global races, tmp-dir session files, eval() numeric oracle
evidence: (1) Suites parsed from human-readable Rust stdout (parse_corpus @940 expects 'Total cases:'/'Passed:' lines; parse_algorithmic_backend_observability regexes a labeled JSON line @3384) — a Rust-side wording change breaks the scorecard, but failure is LOUD (ValueError -> suite fail) and pinned by SAMPLE_* strings in test_engine_improvement_scorecard.py, so this is tax, not risk. (2) server_colab.py mutates module globals session_variables/session_results from ThreadingHTTPServer threads without locks (@237-241, 271, 283) — moot until the startup crash is fixed. (3) server.py writes session snapshots to tempfile.gettempdir()/explicas_cli_sessions with 0644 names derived from sha256(session_id) (@52-53, 153-156) — on shared Linux hosts other users can read them, though ids are browser UUIDs. (4) engine_simplify_equivalence_fuzz.py numeric oracle evals transformed CLI output — trusted local binary, acceptable for a dev harness. (5) main() special-cases suite names as strings (@12549, 12556) — folds into the god-file cleanup.
why: Individually small; recorded so the gaps are visible rather than silently skipped.
fix: (1) covered by the parsers.py extraction; (2) fixed for free by the shared-helper rewrite of server_colab.py; (3) chmod 0600 + per-user subdir; (4) no action; (5) fold into suites.py extraction.

title: Engine matching decisions coupled to cas_formatter display output: expressions compared by rendered-string equality
evidence: calculus_residual_support/mod.rs:44-58 rendered_exprs_match compares format!(DisplayExpr) strings (used at :6666 in variant matching); eval/simplify_action.rs:2868-2893 calculus_residual_step_has_visible_payload_local decides step visibility by comparing DisplayExpr renderings of step.before vs step.after.
why: Any cas_formatter output change (spacing, ordering, localization of operators) silently flips residual-match and step-visibility decisions in the engine — an invisible cross-crate behavioral dependency that a formatter-only PR would never suspect.
fix: Replace with structural comparison (compare_expr / exprs_equivalent) where possible; where render-equality is genuinely the intent (visible-payload check), keep it but add a comment marking the formatter contract and a regression test pinning one rendering pair.

title: Summary of remaining minor findings (stringly builtin dispatch, wire-boundary i18n template table, transform_helpers size, silent registration gates)
evidence: (1) 119 sym_name string comparisons workspace-wide: parent_context.rs:484,552 match 'sin'/'cos'/'tan' by name where ctx.builtin_of()/BuiltinFn is the crate norm (transform_helpers.rs:3810 does the same for sqrt/abs); 45 non-test '"diff"/"integrate"' name matches in cas_engine (transform_helpers.rs:26,37,890-956) dispatch the calculus meta-functions stringly. (2) crates/cas_solver/src/eval_output_presentation_solve_steps/localization.rs (262 lines, read header): deliberate wire-boundary es/en template table — every new/edited solver step description in cas_solver_core silently passes through untranslated unless the table is updated in lockstep (documented design, but a per-description shotgun edit). (3) engine/transform/transform_helpers.rs: 4,102 lines / 130 fns, thematically cohesive (raw diff/integrate target gating) but monolithic; contiguous per-target families are easy sibling-module extractions. (4) Memory-documented (not re-verified this session): adding an eval engine function requires the is_known_eval_engine_function gate (cas_session_core/src/eval.rs:68) + budget-exempt allowlist, both failing silently when missed.
why: Individually small, but all are silent-failure couplings: string dispatch and lockstep tables degrade without compile errors, which is what repeatedly burned prior cycles.
fix: Opportunistic: swap sym_name string matches to builtin_of where a BuiltinFn exists (confirm 'diff'/'integrate' have no BuiltinFn first — if not, centralize the two name consts); add a debug assertion or test that every solver step description template in cas_solver_core has a localization entry; split transform_helpers.rs by target family when next touched.

title: 7,700 lines of inline test modules sitting inside the four god files (trig 2,599; derive_command 3,691; hyperbolic 1,417; solve_backend_local 471)
evidence: #[cfg(test)] boundaries: derive_command.rs:7981 (of 11,672), derive/trig.rs:7976 (of 10,575), derive/hyperbolic.rs:4797 (of 6,214), solve_backend_local.rs:7771 (of 8,242). Combined 7,678 test lines inflating the crate's four largest files; the crate already keeps 20+ suites under tests/, so the convention exists.
why: Inflates the god files past editor/reviewer thresholds and recompiles the heaviest translation units on every test edit; also skews any 'largest file' triage the campaign does. Zero-risk to fix.
fix: Move each inline mod to a sibling `<file>_tests.rs` included via #[cfg(test)] mod at the same visibility (child module keeps private access, so NO visibility changes needed — unlike moving to tests/). One mechanical commit per file, huella untouched (tests only).

title: solve_core_runtime bypasses the SolveBackend trait to reach into solve_backend_local internals, voiding the file's own 'switch implementations without changing call sites' contract
evidence: solve_backend_local.rs:1-5 doc: 'Keeping this indirection local lets us switch implementations without changing call sites'; solve_backend_dispatch.rs routes only through SolveBackend::solve_with_ctx_and_options. But solve_core_runtime.rs:28 calls crate::solve_backend_local::try_solve_periodic_trig_equation directly (the only cross-module reach into the backend, made pub(crate) at solve_backend_local.rs:4909 for this call).
why: Small today, but it is the one hole in an otherwise clean backend seam — and it is the same periodic-trig guard involved in the cas_engine divergence (finding 2); fixing both together closes the seam.
fix: Add the recursive pre-check as a second method (with default no-op) on the SolveBackend trait, or move try_solve_periodic_trig_equation behind a fn re-exported by solve_backend_dispatch. Mechanical, huella-identical.

title: try_solve_abs_equality matches abs via raw sym_name string while 5 sibling handlers in the same file use the typed BuiltinFn::Abs check
evidence: Line 7377: `args.len() == 1 && simplifier.context.sym_name(*fn_id) == "abs"` — the only raw-string builtin match in the crate's non-test code (grep over sym_name == "..."). Same-file siblings use `ctx.is_builtin(*fn_id, BuiltinFn::Abs)` at lines 2685, 4195, 5140, 5653, 5693.
why: If abs identity ever diverges from the literal symbol name (aliasing, canonicalization), this one handler silently stops matching while its siblings keep working — an invisible capability hole in exactly the abs-equation family the audits keep probing.
fix: One-line change to `simplifier.context.is_builtin(*fn_id, BuiltinFn::Abs)`; behavior-identical today, huella 0-delta.

title: Summary of remaining minor observations (wrapper-name length, facade health, non-findings)
evidence: (1) Flow wrapper fn names reach 130+ chars (dispatch_isolation_with_default_isolated_and_negated_entries_and_default_linear_collect_kernels_for_var_and_unified_step_mapper_with_state) — they encode the binding chain in the identifier; collapses naturally with finding 1. (2) Checked-and-clean: cas_solver's pub surface is tight (exactly 5 pub mods — lib.rs:50,229,258,333,338), downstream usage concentrates on session_api (157 refs) and runtime (56); runtime.rs is a deliberate facade; derive/ sibling modules have narrow pub(crate) surfaces; no pub-leakage epidemic found. (3) derive/trig.rs (254 fns) and hyperbolic.rs are large but domain-cohesive with small functions (largest 260 lines); only ~28/208 name-parallel fns after sinh->sin renaming, so they are NOT clones — no merge recommended. (4) sym_name string comparisons are otherwise rare in cas_solver (19 total incl. tests).
why: Recorded so the parent agent knows these were examined and deliberately ranked below the cut, not missed.
fix: No action beyond findings 1-11; wrapper naming resolves itself when the context-struct collapse (finding 1) removes the per-default wrapper layers.

title: LaTeX and plain-display renderers duplicate identical structural-detection helpers verbatim (reciprocal-sqrt, mul-factor collection, unit-fraction, hold-unwrap)
evidence: reciprocal_sqrt_numerator_for_latex (latex_core.rs:953) and reciprocal_sqrt_numerator_for_display (display/expr.rs:941) are line-for-line identical; same for collect_mul_factors, unit_fraction_denominator, unwrap_internal_hold (latex_core.rs:61 and display/expr.rs:813 both re-implement the canonical cas_ast::hold::unwrap_internal_hold at hold.rs:101), is_negative_one_half_exponent, and the format_reciprocal_sqrt_div pair (latex_core.rs:479 / display/expr.rs:1033).
why: Pattern-detection fixes land in one renderer and drift from the other, producing inconsistent CLI-vs-LaTeX output for the same expression (these helpers gate which display form is chosen, e.g. 1/sqrt(x) shapes); it also doubles the audit surface for display-hint bugs.
fix: Extract the pure (&Context, ExprId) -> Option<...> detectors into a shared cas_formatter/src/detect.rs (or cas_ast::views) and have both renderers call it; replace the two local unwrap_internal_hold copies with cas_ast::hold::unwrap_internal_hold. Pure moves, 0-delta huella.

title: resolve_mode_entry threads 15 parameters (7 of them &mut accumulator state) through the recursive resolve family
evidence: resolve_mode_entry (resolve.rs:473) takes 15 params: 3 generic callbacks (Lookup/SameReq/MarkReq) + 7 &mut accumulators (memo, visiting, requires, used_cache, ref_chain, seen_hits, cache_hits); resolve_with_mode_recursive is the 16-param sibling; 6 call sites forward the full list; resolve.rs has 4 more 8-param wrappers (resolve_with_lookup_recursive, resolve_session_refs_with_mode_lookup, ...).
why: Same disease as the solver flows at smaller scale: extending session-reference resolution (new cache semantics, new diagnostics) means editing every signature in the chain; the 7 accumulators are one logical traversal state.
fix: Introduce a ResolveCursor<'a, RequiredItem> struct holding the 7 &mut accumulators (constructed once at the entry point) and pass &mut ResolveCursor; callbacks stay as the 3 generics. Local to one file, behavior-preserving.

title: views.rs bypasses cas_ast's own builtin fast path with 5 string compares ctx.sym_name(fn_id) == "sqrt"
evidence: views.rs:135, :199, :1350, :1428, :1444 all do `ctx.sym_name(*fn_id) == "sqrt"` while the same crate documents the preferred check at expression.rs:809-811 ('// Instead of: ctx.sym_name(*fn_id) == "sqrt"' -> builtin_id/is_builtin, a const cast). BuiltinFn::Sqrt exists in builtin.rs (46 builtins, from_name/lookup).
why: Stringly builtin identity inside the AST crate legitimizes the pattern that then spreads upward (cas_formatter root_style.rs:47, latex_core.rs:1238 do the same); it is also a needless string compare in view code that hot paths (surd analysis, MulParts) call repeatedly.
fix: Mechanical replace of the 5 sites with ctx.is_builtin(*fn_id, BuiltinFn::Sqrt) (helper already exists and hold.rs shows the mixed idiom); optionally sweep the 2 cas_formatter sites in the same cycle.

title: LatexNoRoots: 191-line hand-rolled parallel LaTeX renderer exported pub with zero users in the workspace
evidence: grep across all crates: LatexNoRoots appears only in its own file and the re-export at cas_formatter/src/lib.rs:48; no call sites anywhere (checked including cas_engine/cas_solver/cas_cli). It re-implements expr_to_latex recursion independently of the shared LaTeXRenderer trait (latex_core.rs:86), so it silently drifts (its own negatives/parens/hold handling).
why: Dead public API is pure audit surface: any Expr variant added to cas_ast must be handled here too (non-exhaustive match risk), and its output is untested against the canonical renderer.
fix: Delete the module and the lib.rs:48 re-export (or, if kept for a known external consumer, reimplement as a LaTeXRenderer impl overriding only format_pow so roots stay as exponents).

title: Language (a presentation axis) lives in cas_solver_core::eval_option_axes, coupling every presentation crate to solver internals
evidence: locale.rs:13 `use cas_solver_core::eval_option_axes::Language;`; same import in cas_didactic (7 files: step_payloads.rs, step_payload_render/*, types/substep/methods.rs), cas_session/src/eval_command/session.rs:147 and eval_command/pretty.rs:14, and cas_didactic re-exports it at lib.rs (`pub use cas_solver_core::eval_option_axes::Language`). cas_solver_core deps: ast/formatter/math/parser — it is a solver-machinery crate, not an options/wire crate.
why: i18n work (an active campaign per the Cycle-D memory) must name a solver-core path for a UI enum; cas_api_models (the natural home, dep floor = cas_ast+serde) cannot use the enum and the wire layer round-trips it as strings instead.
fix: Move Language to cas_api_models (or cas_ast) and re-export from cas_solver_core::eval_option_axes for compatibility — one-file move plus a pub use, no call-site churn.

title: Summary of smaller in-scope observations (didactic render fns with 9-12 params; description-prefix protocol; healthy areas)
evidence: (a) cas_didactic timeline render family: render_timeline_filtered_steps 12 params (timeline/simplify_render/steps/loop_steps.rs), render_solve_timeline_document 12 (timeline/solve/document.rs), 6 more at 9-10 — same collapse-into-RenderCtx opportunity as findings 1/7. (b) visible_rule_name_for_step (visible_rule_names.rs:227-263) parses engine description PREFIXES ('Telescoping product:', 'Sum of squares:') as a protocol — sub-case of finding 2. (c) Verified healthy: cas_android_ffi (catch_unwind on every JNI entry, lib.rs:69), cas_session runtime impls are small trait adapters (repl_core_runtime_impls/* max 62 lines), cas_cli routes through cas_solver's typed ReplCommandInput (dispatch/route.rs:9) rather than string sniffing, parser/formatter circular dep is dev-only (Cargo.tomls), cas_didactic's cas_session dep is dev-only.
why: Recorded so the bounded-cycle backlog and the clean areas are both visible; none of these individually warrants its own cycle.

title: pub mod api ('backward-compatible facade') has zero production consumers — every remaining user is a test file or bench in other crates
evidence: grep 'cas_solver::api' across crates/*/src: 0 hits. All uses live in cas_cli/tests/*.rs (16 files: solve, solve_with_display_steps, eval_f64, is_zero, render_conditions_normalized), cas_session/tests/requires_propagation_tests.rs, and cas_engine/benches/profile_cache.rs:22. api.rs is 61 lines re-exporting ~60 names from 5 crates; some exports (number_theory::compute_gcd, GcdResult at api.rs:60; RuleSolveSafetyExt) have zero external references at all. lib.rs:378 also consumes it internally, so it does double duty.
why: The doc comment says the facade exists 'during migration' — the migration is complete for production code, and the facade keeps ~60 names artificially externally-reachable, silencing dead_code analysis for their whole call chains (e.g. the confirmed-dead items above sit behind exactly this pattern).
fix: Demote: point the test/bench imports at session_api/command_api (or the owning crates), then shrink api.rs to the handful of names tests genuinely need, or mark it #[doc(hidden)] with a removal note. Prune the zero-reference exports (compute_gcd/GcdResult/RuleSolveSafetyExt) immediately.

title: Stale #[cfg_attr(not(test), allow(dead_code))] labels the LIVE production `subst` path as production-dead
evidence: substitute/eval.rs:7 marks evaluate_substitute_and_simplify, substitute/parse.rs:5,28 mark parse_substitute_args, substitute.rs:25 marks SubstituteSimplifyEvalOutput — all claiming test-only. But the traced production path is: cas_cli/src/repl/commands_analysis/symbolic.rs:28 → session_api::substitute::evaluate_substitute_invocation_user_message_on_repl_core → repl_simplifier_runtime/symbolic.rs:14-19 → substitute_command_eval/invocation.rs → substitute_command_eval/eval.rs:11 which calls evaluate_substitute_and_simplify → parse_substitute_args. The annotations are the inverse of reality.
why: Memory documents that audit scouts judge liveness from exactly these annotations ('scout reads steps=Vec::new() and concludes ~0%'); a wrong test-only label on the REPL substitute entry point will misdirect the next substitution-capability audit.
fix: Delete the four cfg_attr(not(test), allow(dead_code))/allow(unused_imports) attributes in src/substitute.rs, substitute/eval.rs, substitute/parse.rs; the compiler will confirm the items are live.

title: pathsteps_to_expr_path in domain_facade.rs is a dead duplicate of cas_solver_core::step_types::pathsteps_to_expr_path
evidence: domain_facade.rs:51-55: '#[allow(dead_code)] pub fn pathsteps_to_expr_path(...)' — zero callers in the workspace (only lib.rs:423's allow(unused_imports) re-export names it). All real consumers (cas_engine/src/step.rs:7, cas_didactic/src/lib.rs:20, step_recording.rs:74,98) use the cas_solver_core::step_types version, which is byte-for-byte the same map over PathStep::to_child_index.
why: Duplicate path-conversion helpers are a divergence hazard for the didactic step-path machinery the solve-steps work depends on; the allow(dead_code) guarantees nobody notices it exists.
fix: Delete domain_facade.rs:50-55 and remove pathsteps_to_expr_path from the lib.rs:421-424 import block.

title: LimitResult.steps is a dead field: always constructed as Vec::new(), never read, hidden by #[allow(dead_code)]
evidence: limit.rs:11-13 '#[allow(dead_code)] pub steps: Vec<Step>'; the only constructor (limit(), line 27-31) hardcodes 'steps: Vec::new()'; sole consumer limit_command_core/core.rs:22 reads only .expr and .warning. title: 'Parse error:' is a stringly-typed protocol: 7+ producer format! sites re-parsed by prefix matching at two consumer sites
evidence: Producers hand-format the prefix in cas_solver: rationalize_command.rs:39, assignment_format.rs:25, parse_error_render.rs:43, solve_command_errors.rs:4 and :15, eval_input/build.rs:18, eval_input/build/statement.rs:11, wire/envelope.rs:35. Consumers classify by string prefix: cas_api_models/src/wire_types.rs:324 `error.starts_with("Parse error:")` (drives wire kind=ParseError/code=E_PARSE) and cas_solver/src/eval_command_text.rs:42. cas_solver_core/src/error_model.rs:21 already has a typed `#[error("Parse error: {0}")]` variant that these bypass.
why: The wire error taxonomy — which the smoke matrices and any frontend depend on — silently degrades to kind=Error if any one producer rewords, localizes (the es/en i18n round is active), or lowercases the prefix. Parallel table maintained by hand across crate boundaries.
fix: Route all producers through one constructor (cas_solver/src/parse_error_render.rs is the natural canonical) or better, carry a typed error kind to the wire boundary and derive the string there, keeping today's exact strings so the wire payloads and huella stay identical.

title: Command-matrix smoke harness (~10 functions) copied across diff/integrate/limit scripts with classifier drift
evidence: engine_diff_command_matrix_smoke.py, engine_integrate_command_matrix_smoke.py, engine_limit_command_matrix_smoke.py each define the same harness set with identical signatures: parse_args, terminate_process_group, parse_json (identical `def parse_json(stdout: str) -> tuple[dict[str, Any] | None, str | None]` in all three), extract_required_display, extract_warning_messages, extract_step_text, warning_expectations_met, classify_error_kind, run_case, count_by. classify_error_kind has drifted: the diff copy (line 1529) has a 'blocked hint' -> blocked_hint_mismatch branch the limit copy (line 2931) lacks (falls to 'unknown'); the integrate copy adds antiderivative branches; engine_calculus_residual_probe_smoke.py has a fourth structurally different variant.
why: These scripts ARE the validation harness (scorecard huella suites); drift in error classification means the same failure mode is counted under different issue kinds per suite, muddying exactly the per-suite counters the memory says to judge huellas by.
fix: Extract a shared scripts/command_matrix_harness.py with the common runner/JSON helpers and a base classifier that per-script classifiers extend (diff/integrate branches layered on top, preserving current branch ORDER per script so emitted counters stay byte-identical). Validate with a stash-regenerate huella comparison before/after.

evidence: (a) `expr_contains_structural` twice with drift: rules/functions.rs:54 does NOT descend Hold; rules/trigonometry/identities/tan_half_angle_rules.rs:84 handles `Neg(inner) | Hold(inner)` (same Hold-drift class as finding 2). (b) `expr_eq` (compare_expr == Equal) defined at orchestrator.rs:1529, calculus_residual_support/mod.rs:12, fraction_residual_support.rs:15, plus expr_eq_fast at rules/algebra/mod.rs:58. (c) cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:762 `expr_contains_named_var` allocates via collect_variables while the same file already imports the early-exit cas_solver_core::isolation_utils::contains_var (used at line ~5507); test-only re-derivations in cas_math/src/substitute.rs:412 and expr_path_rewrite.rs:188. (d) `expr_contains_named_function_local` near-copies: orchestrator.rs:801 (single name) vs eval/simplify_action.rs:2553 (name slice). NON-FINDING kept for the record: affine_coefficients (cas_solver/solve_backend_local.rs:5500, sampling-based, needs Simplifier) vs affine_var_rational_slope (cas_solver_core/isolation_arithmetic.rs:1299, structural, core-layer) implement different algorithms with documented, layer-driven reasons (core has no Simplifier) — deliberate layering, not accidental drift.

title: Four hand-synced CLI command/function tables with verified drift (weierstrass, cache, autoexpand missing; completer functions list stale subset of engine registry)
evidence: Command names live in 4 places: the dispatcher (repl/dispatch/route.rs handles Weierstrass/Cache/etc. via cas_solver's parser), completer.rs:16-70 commands vec, repl/help_topics.rs match, repl/general_help.rs text. Verified drift: 'weierstrass' dispatchable but absent from completer/help_topics/general_help (grep counts 0/0/0); 'cache' absent from completer; 'autoexpand' absent from help_topics. The completer functions vec (completer.rs:71-106) is a stale subset of the canonical registries (cas_ast::BuiltinFn ALL_BUILTINS + cas_session_core::eval::is_known_eval_engine_function at eval.rs:68): missing asin/acos/sinh/cosh/exp/log/floor/ceil/taylor/apart etc.
why: Every new engine function or REPL command (routine during a universality campaign, and already a documented gotcha in memory: 'new engine function wiring gotchas') requires touching up to 4 tables; misses fail silently as missing completion/help.
fix: Derive the completer's function list from cas_ast::ALL_BUILTINS + a public iterator over the is_known_eval_engine_function names, and add a test asserting every ReplCommandInput keyword appears in completer + general_help (help_topics may stay curated but should fall back gracefully).

title: Two identical wire span DTOs with swapped names (WireSpan vs SpanWire) and duplicated error-code literals across two wire families in one crate
evidence: wire.rs:290 `pub struct WireSpan { start, end }` and wire_types.rs:1169 `pub struct SpanWire { start, end }` both mirror cas_ast::Span. Error codes E_PARSE/E_INTERNAL/E_INVALID_INPUT are built two ways: WireMsg::parse_error embeds the code in a json data blob (wire.rs:112-116) while EngineWireError::parse/simple/invalid_input use typed kind/code fields (wire_types.rs:1216-1246); cas_android_ffi re-hardcodes the InternalError/E_INTERNAL pair (lib.rs:98).
why: Two parallel wire protocols (REPL-message wire vs engine wire) in the same crate with near-anagram type names is a standing confusion tax and lets error-code wording drift between the REPL JSON and the FFI JSON for the same failure.
fix: Unify on one span DTO (keep SpanWire, add serde Deserialize, alias WireSpan = SpanWire during a deprecation window) and route WireMsg::parse_error's code constants through the EngineWireError constants. Do NOT merge the two envelope schemas (both are versioned boundaries); just share the leaf types/literals.

title: solve_backend_local.rs uses two different contains-variable helpers interchangeably and re-implements flip_inequality locally
evidence: expr_contains_named_var (:762) wraps cas_ast::collect_variables — allocating a HashSet<String> of ALL variables per boolean query — and is used at ~14 sites (:804, :823, :843, :953, :1734, :2065, :3013, :3040, :5905 ...), while the same file imports the early-exit cas_solver_core::isolation_utils::contains_var at 27+ sites (:2243, :2426, :3245 ...); which one a handler uses is historical accident. flip_inequality (:910) is a verbatim re-implementation of the pub cas_solver_core::isolation_utils::flip_inequality (:65), semantics identical (Eq/Neq pass through).

Consistency here is cheap insurance for the file most touched by the soundness campaign.

title: phase_runtime_* trio triplicated with a real p95-formula drift: two different percentile definitions publish the same p95_case_ms metric
evidence: phase_runtime_distribution/case_rows/observability_summary (~130 lines) exist in engine_diff_command_matrix_smoke.py:1837/1861/1911, engine_limit_command_matrix_smoke.py:3200/3224/3266, engine_integrate_command_matrix_smoke.py:6810/6772/7287. The shared module engine_command_matrix_observability.py:90 and the diff copy (diff:1851) use nearest-rank `max(0, (95*n+99)//100 - 1)` with the comment 'Nearest-rank percentile keeps the signal stable for small smoke matrices'; the limit (3213) and integrate (6823) copies use `min(n-1, int(n*0.95))` — for n=20 these pick index 18 vs 19, so the SAME metric key p95_case_ms means different statistics per lane, while engine_improvement_scorecard.py:50 applies one uniform threshold CALCULUS_RUNTIME_PRESSURE_RISK_P95_MS=250.0 to all of them (risk/watch signals at scorecard lines 5578-5583). title: classify_error_kind is a hand-synced string table matched against error literals produced in other functions/modules

title: run_case Popen/timeout/timing skeleton re-implemented in each of the 4 calculus smoke lanes
evidence: run_case in diff:1553 (284 lines), limit:2953 (247 lines), integrate:5726 (375 lines) plus run_probe in residual:1033 share an identical skeleton: `subprocess.Popen(..., start_new_session=True)` + `communicate(timeout=...)` + `except TimeoutExpired: terminate_process_group(...)` + identical timeout-result dict + identical cli_*_elapsed_seconds derivation block (diff:1596-1614 verbatim in the others). diff-vs-limit shows 225 changed lines, i.e. the divergence is the per-lane expected-field assertions, not the process handling.

title: Bench save/compare triples repeat the same filter literal 3x each, inconsistently with the file's own variable pattern; .PHONY list already drifted
evidence: ~25 target triples repeat the identical cargo-bench invocation with the filter string inlined 3 times, e.g. 'repl_individual/cached/(03_|04_|06_|08_|10_)' at Makefile:343, 347, 351 and the long session-phase regex at 404, 408, 412 — while other triples already do it right via variables (SOLVE_BATCH_FILTER line 3, VERIFY_BENCH_FILTER line 4, used at 306-315 and 359-368) or via delegation (bench-engine-solve-hotspots-* at 300-304 delegate to bench-engine-fast-*-seq).
