title: Quadratic-root computation implemented three times inside cas_solver_core
evidence: quadratic_roots_with_leading (domain_normalization.rs:2638): Polynomial::from_expr + discriminant b^2-4ac + rational_sqrt + (-b±sqrt)/2a. solve_rational_quadratic (solve_outcome.rs:781): same discriminant arithmetic plus normalize_quadratic_coeffs(:70)/split_square_factor(:103)/build_surd_roots(:125) for surd roots. quadratic_formula.rs: discriminant(:73)/discriminant_expr(:63)/roots_from_a_b_delta(:38) as the didactic plan machinery. Read all three; the rational-discriminant arithmetic is repeated verbatim in the first two.
why: A change in root policy (e.g. how a zero discriminant or non-primitive triple is handled) must be found in three places; the domination-check copy silently declines surd roots (rational_sqrt-only), so domain proofs are weaker than the solver on the same quadratic.
fix: Have quadratic_roots_with_leading and solve_rational_quadratic share one exact rational/surd root helper (natural home: next to quadratic_formula::discriminant, or cas_math); keep the didactic expr-building layer separate.

title: RelOp flip helper triplicated (one copy in the same crate as the canonical public one)
evidence: flip_relop (solve_outcome.rs:156, private) is behaviorally identical to the public isolation_utils::flip_inequality (isolation_utils.rs:65) in the SAME crate; a third private copy at cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:910 (cas_solver already depends on cas_solver_core and imports isolation_utils elsewhere). All three read and compared: same 4-arrow match, Eq/Neq passthrough.
why: Trivial today, but it is a semantic table (inequality direction under negation) that soundness depends on; three copies invite a subtle divergence if Neq/Eq handling ever changes.
fix: Delete flip_relop and the cas_solver copy; call cas_solver_core::isolation_utils::flip_inequality. One-commit cleanup, huella-identical.

title: Exact rational square-root check re-implemented despite public cas_math::perfect_square_support::rational_sqrt
evidence: domain_condition.rs:744 rational_sqrt_nonnegative re-implements BigInt::sqrt + verify-square for numer/denom. Canonical public API exists at cas_math/src/perfect_square_support.rs:51 rational_sqrt and is ALREADY used by this crate (solve_outcome.rs solve_rational_quadratic imports it). Two more private copies: cas_math/src/const_sign.rs:109 exact_sqrt and cas_math/src/factor.rs:401 rational_sqrt.
why: Pure maintenance duplication; the four copies are consistent today but each new numeric edge (zero, negative-zero denom normalization) must be checked in four places.
fix: Replace domain_condition.rs:744 with a call to cas_math::perfect_square_support::rational_sqrt; separately (out of this crate) fold the two cas_math-internal copies into the same function.

title: Squarefree-factor extraction duplicated with DIVERGENT trial-division caps (100000 vs 97), so the same radicand renders differently on different fronts
evidence: solve_outcome.rs:103 split_square_factor: trial division cap = BigInt 100_000. domain_condition.rs:834 split_small_square_factor: loop `for factor in 2..=97`. A third copy at cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/scalar_rational_sqrt_presentation.rs:49. Same algorithm, hand-diverged bounds: sqrt(101^2*2) simplifies to 101*sqrt(2) in solution roots but stays sqrt(20402) in domain-condition display strings.
why: A concrete, already-existing behavioral drift between two copies of one algorithm — exactly the class of inconsistency that confuses didactic output and makes solution/domain rendering disagree.
fix: Move one capped split_square_factor (parameterized cap, default 100_000) into cas_math (next to perfect_square_support) and call it from all three sites.

title: Summary of remaining verified minor duplications (contains_var twin, get_number vs as_rational_const, mul_leaves twin in cas_math)
evidence: (1) cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:762 expr_contains_named_var re-implements isolation_utils.rs:14 contains_var via cas_ast::collect_variables().iter().any() — semantically equivalent but allocates the full variable set per call; cas_solver already depends on and imports isolation_utils::contains_var (solve_backend_local.rs:5507). (2) solution_set.rs:28 get_number (Number/Neg only) is a weaker sibling of cas_math::numeric_eval::as_rational_const, used in the SAME file that also imports as_rational_const (:231) — compare_values' Number branch is thus blinder than the surd branches below it. (3) cas_math has a private mul_leaves at expr_relations.rs:330 shadowing the public expr_nary.rs:537 one (out of core scope, noted for the owner). Error-message mapping (seed 5) checked: solve_runtime_mapping.rs is small and centralized in-scope — no duplicated table found in cas_solver_core.
why: Each is a small tax individually; together they are the same pattern feeding the larger findings — private siblings of existing public helpers that future fixes will miss.
fix: Point expr_contains_named_var at isolation_utils::contains_var; replace solution_set::get_number's use in compare_values with as_rational_const (strictly widens the exact Number branch — verify with huella); fold the cas_math mul_leaves twin.

title: dedup_and_sort sorts required conditions by display Strings rebuilt inside the comparator despite having just computed those exact keys for dedup
evidence: diagnostics_model.rs:133 computes `let key = item.cond.display(ctx)` per item for the dedup HashMap, then line 143 `merged.sort_by(|a, b| a.cond.display(ctx).cmp(&b.cond.display(ctx)))` re-renders TWO display Strings (full expression tree walk + allocation) per comparison, discarding the keys built ten lines earlier. 8 call sites across the pipeline (cas_engine/src/eval/diagnostics.rs:730, cas_solver eval/derive/solve command runtimes), once per user-visible result.
why: Same format-string-sorting pattern as the known cas_ast ConditionSet seed, in a core shared type (Diagnostics); bounded n keeps it P3, but it is a two-line fix and removes O(n log n) tree renders per output.
fix: Carry the (key, item) pairs from the dedup pass and sort_by on the cached key (or use sort_by_cached_key with the display String).

title: Interval-algebra sorting/merging re-runs exact surd decomposition and BigRational interval bounds per comparison with no per-endpoint key caching
evidence: merge_intervals sorts with `intervals.sort_by(|a, b| compare_values(ctx, a.min, b.min))` (770) and calls compare_values twice more per merge step (776, 786). compare_values (36-102) per call: is_infinity x4, get_number x2, as_surd_value/compare_quadratic_surds (BigRational surd normal form), compare_nth_root_surds, then cas_math const_value_bounds on BOTH endpoints (exact interval arithmetic over the whole constant subtree). The same endpoint is re-analyzed O(log n) times during sort and again during merge; nothing is cached.
why: union/intersect_solution_sets have 66 call sites and the Periodic-union work keeps growing interval lists; each new exact-comparison rung added for soundness (surds, nth roots, transcendentals — all added recently per the comments) multiplies the per-comparison cost.
fix: Precompute a sort key per unique endpoint ExprId (Option<BigRational> value or bounds pair) into a small HashMap before sorting, and have compare_values consult it; keeps exact semantics (huella-safe).

title: Aggregate: 70 ctx.get(id).clone() sites and rebuild-without-change recursions (substitute_named_var, normalize_expr_for_display) cause avoidable BigRational/Vec clones and interner churn
evidence: 70 grep hits of `get(...).clone()` in src/ (e.g. substitution.rs:1196, eval_step_pipeline.rs:276/425/488/531, isolation_utils.rs:50-53, rational_roots.rs:34, log_linear_narration.rs:151/254/291); cloning an Expr node copies a BigRational (Number) or Vec<ExprId> (Function/Matrix) even when a match-on-reference + child-id copies suffice. substitute_named_var (1195-1254) additionally re-interns EVERY node of unchanged subtrees (no `if a_sub == a && b_sub == b { return expr }` short-circuit and no contains-var pruning), paying a hash+interner lookup per node per substitution; same shape in eval_step_pipeline::normalize_expr_for_display:276.
why: Individually cold-to-warm, collectively a steady allocation tax across isolation, narration and verification paths; also the pattern keeps getting copy-pasted (the newest files have it), so naming it once stops the spread before the campaign adds more handlers.
fix: In the recursions, destructure child ids by reference first and only clone payload-bearing variants when actually needed; add unchanged-children short-circuits (return the original ExprId) in substitute_named_var and normalize_expr_for_display.

title: Smoke matrices and the scorecard run every case/suite strictly serially, one fresh cas_cli process per probe (up to 4 per case), with zero parallelism anywhere in the harness
evidence: run_matrix is a serial list comprehension over 313 IntegrateCommandMatrixCase entries (scripts/engine_integrate_command_matrix_smoke.py:7390-7398), each run_case spawning 1-4 Popen calls (lines 5735, 5834, 5888, 5999); limit matrix has 209 cases, diff 85, residual probe 25 — grep for ThreadPool/multiprocessing/concurrent across scripts/ returns nothing. The scorecard main loop runs suites serially too (engine_improvement_scorecard.py:12522). Measured from committed artifacts: guardrail scorecard sums 88.4s, fast profile 141.5s (docs/generated/engine_improvement_scorecard*.json, process_elapsed_seconds), and the workflow regenerates scorecards multiple times per improvement cycle (baseline + candidate + stash-regenerate huella checks).
why: The scorecard gate is the inner loop of every improvement cycle; minutes per regeneration multiply across the universality campaign. However: per-case elapsed metrics feed compute_embedded_runtime_guardrail (elapsed-vs-baseline regression flags) and per-case cli_*_us fields, so naive parallelism adds latency noise to fields the workflow already treats as noisy — hence P3, not P2.
fix: Bounded option: add an opt-in `--jobs N` ProcessPoolExecutor to the three matrix smokes only (their pass/fail counters are latency-independent; keep wall_elapsed fields but mark them non-huella, which memory says they already are). Leave cargo-based suites serial to avoid target-dir lock contention.

title: Per-request O((functions+variables) x expr_len) regex substitution passes over expressions up to 500K chars
evidence: eval_with_substitution runs one full `re.sub` pass over the whole expression per defined function (lines 812-814) plus one per session variable (lines 817-826), on every eval; expressions can be up to the 500000 `--max-chars` scale and imported notebooks can carry many variables/functions. Each pass rescans the entire string; with e.g. 50 vars on a 500K expr that is 25 MB of scanning per request on the single server thread.
why: Cold for typical interactive use (few vars, short exprs) but sits on the only thread of the primary eval path and degrades exactly in the import-heavy sessions the ref_map machinery was built to speed up.
fix: Build one combined alternation regex (`\b(name1|name2|...)\b` sorted longest-first) with a dict-lookup replacement callback — single pass regardless of variable count. Only worth doing when touching the file for the P2 fixes.

title: DivExpandToCancel rule attempt recomputes find_shared_opaque_calls and the broad-opaque no-match scan twice within one flow
evidence: try_rewrite_div_expand_to_cancel_expr_with calls should_skip_broad_opaque_root_expand_cancel(ctx,num,den,4,3) at line 804 (which runs find_shared_opaque_calls + broad_opaque_root_expand_cancel_no_match, lines 452-456), then immediately calls try_opaque_substitution_cancel_with (line 807) which re-runs find_shared_opaque_calls (line 652) and broad_opaque_root_expand_cancel_no_match (line 670) on the same num/den. find_shared_opaque_calls does two full collect_function_calls_with_pow_limit walks plus O(num_calls × den_calls) structural matching with compare_expr. DivExpandToCancelRule is a registered simplifier rule (cas_engine/src/rules/algebra/mod.rs:242) attempted on Div nodes in the rewrite loop; the function also clones the Context per attempt (line 657) after the shared-calls gate.
why: Doubled full-tree scans plus a Context clone per Div-node rule attempt adds up in fraction-heavy simplifications, and the duplication invites divergence between the skip-check and the attempt path.
fix: Compute `shared` once in the orchestration function and pass &shared into both try_opaque_substitution_cancel_with and the no-match check (change signatures to accept the precomputed Vec).

title: collect_impl groups Add terms by O(k²) pairwise deep structural comparison
evidence: collect_terms.rs:58-76: for each flattened term, a linear scan over all existing groups calls are_structurally_equal → cas_ast::ordering::compare_expr (a deep tree comparison) per pair — O(k² · term-size) for a k-term sum. Consumed by collect_semantics_support.rs:89 → cas_engine/src/collect_rule_support.rs:36 and collect.rs:37, i.e. the engine's collect-like-terms rules. Also extract_numerical_coeff at line 143 does ctx.get(expr).clone() (clones a BigRational when the term is a bare Number).
why: Collecting like terms over wide sums is exactly what happens after polynomial expansion (a universality-campaign staple, e.g. expand-then-collect of (a+b+c)^n with 100+ terms → 10k+ deep comparisons per pass).
fix: Bucket groups by a cheap key first (semantic/structural hash of term_part in a HashMap, falling back to compare_expr only within a bucket); keeps output order via the existing final sort.

title: collect_mul_factors_int_pow aggregates repeated bases via O(k²) iter().find with deep compare_expr
evidence: fraction_factors.rs:78-90: for each collected factor, `aggregated.iter_mut().find(...)` runs compare_expr against every existing entry. Callers include the per-term loop of try_rewrite_div_add_common_factor_from_den_expr (div_add_common_factor_from_den_support.rs:37-41, once per Add term) — which itself is invoked from the orchestrator (orchestrator.rs:662), the rationalize rule (rules/algebra/fractions/rationalize.rs:30), and (after the P1 gate fix) the semantic-equality path. cancel_common_factor_vectors_with (lines 367-409) is similarly O(num_factors × den_factors) compare_expr but that is inherent to the algorithm.
why: Compounds multiplicatively with its callers' per-term loops in fraction cancellation, a hot rewrite family; cheap to bound.
fix: Since ids are hash-consed, check `*existing == base` first (already done) and short-circuit compare_expr behind a per-entry cheap discriminant (node tag + child ids); or aggregate via HashMap keyed by ExprId with a small fallback list for non-identical-but-equal bases.

title: check_semantic_equality has no depth budget and retries both orientations for commutative Add/Mul with no pair memoization (worst-case exponential)
evidence: semantic_equality.rs:193-205: Add/Mul compare tries (l1,l2)&(r1,r2) then (l1,r2)&(r1,l2) — up to 4 recursive comparisons per node pair, re-walking the same subtrees; no depth counter anywhere in check_semantic_equality (contrast: prove_sign and as_rational_const both carry depth budgets), and no (a,b) memo. Pow exponents additionally invoke try_evaluate_numeric (full as_rational_const subtree evals, lines 215-220) per visit. Mitigation in practice: the a==b fast path (line 70) hits often because the arena hash-conses identical subtrees.
why: are_equal runs per applied rewrite (rule_application.rs:142) and inside step filtering; a near-symmetric pair of large sums whose halves are semantically equal but structurally distinct ids can trigger the 4-way retry cascade — an adversarial-input hang class the campaign's sweeps could hit. Confirmation would need a constructed benchmark (e.g. two k-level Add trees of pairwise-equal-but-distinct-id leaves) showing super-linear growth.
fix: Add a depth/work budget (return false on exhaustion — sound for this caller: false only means 'keep the rewrite'), and/or memoize compared (min,max) ExprId pairs in a small HashSet per checker instance.

title: const_value_bounds re-runs as_rational_const at every recursion level (O(n²) full-subtree evals within a single bounds computation)
evidence: const_value_bounds_depth (const_sign.rs:403-415) calls as_rational_const(ctx, expr) at EVERY node before matching; as_rational_const (numeric_eval.rs:29-68) recursively evaluates the whole subtree with BigRational arithmetic. For a constant expression with a k-node rational spine plus one irrational leaf, the top-level eval does O(k) work then fails, the child repeats on O(k-1), etc. — O(k²) exact-arithmetic evaluations per bounds query. Same pattern at root_forms.rs:1772 (as_linear_surd). Constant expressions are usually small, so this is a multiplier on findings 3/4 rather than standalone.
why: It sits under every sign-guard and endpoint comparison; fixing it once (bounded) removes a quadratic factor from the whole exact decision layer as more oracle consumers are added.
fix: Evaluate bottom-up: let the recursion return an enum {Point(BigRational), Interval(lo,hi)} so rational subtrees are computed exactly once (the point-interval case already exists — just stop re-probing with as_rational_const at each level).

title: are_equivalent simplifies the identical diff expression twice in one flow
evidence: equivalence.rs:35-43 simplifies `diff` when a calculus call is present; if that didn't prove zero, the fallback at line 67 runs `self.simplify(diff)` again on the SAME ExprId — a full fresh pipeline (each simplify() spins a new transformer; no cross-call cache), after the expand(diff) pipeline at line 47 already ran.
why: Verification paths (solve result checking, equivalence probes) triple-run pipelines on calculus-bearing inputs; pure waste on a path that universality audits exercise heavily.
fix: Remember the first (direct_diff) result in a local and reuse it in the fallback branch.

title: integrate() preserve-guards clone the whole Context arena per integrate-node visit
evidence: transform_helpers.rs:3068, 3082, 3095, 3108 — four `let mut probe_ctx = ctx.clone();` inside integrate_target_is_* boolean predicates, chained from integrate_call_should_preserve_raw_target_for_direct_integration (transform_helpers.rs:~32-80, guard calls at lines 58-61). The chain re-runs every time an integrate() Function node is transformed, i.e., per phase per fixpoint iteration, and the four clones run whenever the earlier guards in the || chain return false.
why: Integration is a flagship front for the completeness campaign; per-visit arena clones make integrate-heavy sessions pay O(arena) repeatedly just to answer a shape question.
fix: Make the cas_math symbolic_integration_support probes take &Context (they only classify shape), or cache the boolean verdict per ExprId for the pass.

title: transform_expr_recursive double-clones Function args (and clones the Expr) on every node visit
evidence: transform/mod.rs:273 `let expr = self.context.get(id).clone();` clones the node (heap Vec for Function/Matrix variants), then line 291 `Expr::Function(fn_id, args) => self.transform_function(id, fn_id, args.clone())` clones the args Vec AGAIN even though the match arm already owns it from the line-273 clone.
why: Redundant Vec allocation per Function node in the single hottest traversal; trivially avoidable.
fix: Pass the owned `args` (drop `.clone()`); optionally restructure the match to copy the two ExprIds for binary ops without cloning the whole Expr.

title: Summary of remaining smaller observations (unbounded sibling loops, unbounded exact pow, minor string dedup)
evidence: (a) Three more uncapped BigInt trial-division loops: number_theory_support.rs:642, 968, 1243 (factor/divisor helpers) and logarithm_inverse_support.rs:503 — same hang exposure as finding 1 when reachable from simplification of log/root forms with large literals. (b) power_eval_support.rs:175 `Pow::pow(&outside_rat, pow_num.unsigned_abs())` — exact BigRational exponentiation with no magnitude bound on the result (pattern f). (c) equivalence.rs:128,163,236 dedups `requires` via `Vec<String>::contains` per step×condition — O(n²) string compares, small n in practice. (d) SemanticEqualityChecker::are_equal explores both orderings for Add/Mul with no ExprId memo (semantic_equality.rs:194-206), O(n²) worst case on mismatched balanced trees — acceptable today but worth a memo if equality checks grow.
why: Individually minor or cold, but (a) shares the exact hang mechanism of the P1 and should be fixed in the same bounded cycle.
fix: Apply the same divisor/bit-size cap as finding 1 to the three sibling loops; add a bits-based bound before exact pow; leave (c)/(d) unless profiling shows them.

title: sort_signed_terms_for_compare sorts by a String key that fully renders the expression, recomputed on every comparison
evidence: focused_rule_substeps.rs:21470-21477: `terms.sort_by_key(|(expr, sign)| { ... (sign_key, display_expr(ctx, *expr)) })` — sort_by_key does NOT cache keys, so each comparison renders BOTH expressions to full display Strings (display_expr at :21090 formats via DisplayExpr, which itself collects+sorts Add terms per fmt). The sibling helper signed_additive_term_multiset_matches at :3289-3296 does the same job correctly with compare_expr (no allocation).
why: Same string-keyed-sort disease as the domain.rs seed, in the didactic layer; harmless today (small term counts, one call site at :21421-21422) but it is the pattern a sweep should eradicate so it doesn't get copied into hotter code.
fix: Replace with the compare_expr-based comparator already used at :3289 (sign key via then_with), or at minimum sort_by_cached_key. Huella-safe if display order and compare_expr order agree for the affected substep outputs — verify; otherwise keep ordering via sort_by_cached_key which is behavior-identical.

title: Context::num allocates a fresh BigRational (heap BigInts) on every call just to hash-lookup an already-interned constant
evidence: expression.rs:738-745: `pub fn num(&mut self, n: i64) { self.add(Expr::Number(BigRational::from_integer(BigInt::from(n)))) }` — num-bigint BigInt heap-allocates even for 0/1; after the interner hit the allocation is dropped. 2370 static call sites of `ctx.num(0)`/`ctx.num(1)` alone across the workspace (many inside rule bodies and normalize_expr_for_display's zero/one folding, i.e. the rule-application loop). expr_hash has an i64 fast path (:168-170) but the BigRational construction cost remains.
why: Constant churn (2 allocs + hash + drop per call) multiplied across millions of rule evaluations; trivial to eliminate and shrinks allocator noise in the exact loops a universality campaign stresses.
fix: Cache ExprIds for -1, 0, 1, 2 as Context fields populated lazily (or in Context::new) and early-return in num(); huella-safe (same ids, same behavior).

title: Minor cold-path inefficiencies bundle: triple nested-fraction classification per step, per-substep re-cleaning of identical strings, degree recomputation in display sorts, per-node Expr clones in tree rebuilds
evidence: (a) rule_dispatch.rs:72-83 is_nested_fraction_step lowercases rule_name+description and runs classify_nested_fraction up to 3x for EVERY step regardless of rule (classifier is shallow but contains_division_like_term walks the tree). (b) step_loop.rs:57-95 prune_redundant_substeps calls clean_display_string (~20 substring scans each) on the same before/after strings twice (retain closure + single-substep block), and prune_duplicate_snapshot_substeps (:251-268) cleans them a third time. (c) cas_formatter/src/display/ordering.rs:94-137 cmp_term_for_display recomputes poly_degree_fast + ln_power_display_degree on every comparison inside terms.sort_by at display/expr.rs:39, styled.rs:116, hints.rs:88, latex_core.rs:186 (no cached keys). (d) cas_session_core/src/env.rs:330 and resolve.rs rebuild trees with `ctx.get(expr).clone()` per node (BigRational/Vec clone per Number/Function node) without ExprId memoization on shared DAG nodes — fine for user-sized inputs, quadratic-to-exponential only on pathological shared shapes. All are presentation/session paths, none plausibly dominates.
why: Individually negligible; listed so the bounded-cycle sweep that fixes the P2s knows these were seen and triaged as not-worth-a-cycle, and to prevent them being re-reported by future audits.
fix: Only if touching these files anyway: gate is_nested_fraction_step behind the cheap rule-name checks before classifying after; hoist cleaned strings in prune_redundant_substeps; use sort_by_cached_key with a (degree, ln_degree, neg) tuple in cmp_term_for_display call sites.

title: Finite sum/product stages clone the whole Context only to unconditionally overwrite the original with the mutated clone
evidence: try_run_finite_sum_target_aware_stage (derive_command.rs:6744-6754): `temp.context = simplifier.context.clone(); ... simplify ...; simplifier.context = temp.context;` — the assignment back is unconditional, so the original context value is always discarded and the clone protects nothing. Identical pattern in try_run_finite_product_target_aware_stage at 6801-6811.
why: Pure waste of a full arena copy on every finite-sum/product derive stage; trivially removable without any behavior change.
fix: `temp.context = std::mem::take(&mut simplifier.context);` (Context implements Default) and move it back after — zero clones. Huella-safe by construction.

title: Commutative multiset matching is O(n^2) pairs where each pair runs the full heavy factors_match battery
evidence: commutative_mul_multiset_match (match_support.rs:233-249) and commutative_add_multiset_match (268-284) do nested loops with `factors_match` per pair; factors_match (298-327) per invocation runs normalized_structural_match (2x normalize_core), projected_root_power_match (2x projection + 2x normalize_core), and TWO SemanticEqualityChecker passes (which can hit the P1 context-clone arm). For an n-term sum that ultimately fails to match, this is up to n^2 x (heavy battery) work.
why: Runs inside the 514-call-site match battery; n is usually small (2-6 terms) so this is a multiplier on the other match_support findings rather than a standalone blowup.
fix: Pre-normalize each term once into a Vec<(normalized ExprId, sign)> and match by sorted compare_expr order (O(n log n)), falling back to the pairwise battery only for terms whose normalized ids differ. Bounded to this file.

title: Variable-membership checks allocate a full HashSet<String> of all variable names instead of using the existing early-out contains_var
evidence: solve_backend_local.rs:3090 and 3130 call `cas_ast::collect_variables(ctx, a).contains(var)`; collect_variables (cas_ast/src/traversal.rs:157-170) walks the whole subtree inserting `.to_string()` per Variable node into a HashSet, with no early exit. A purpose-built early-out `contains_var(ctx, expr, var)` already exists (cas_solver_core/src/isolation_utils.rs:14) and is used elsewhere in the same file (e.g. line 6285). Site 3130 sits inside `contains_trig_of_var`, which recurses over every Function node — O(F x subtree) with String allocations — and additionally clones the args Vec per visited Function node (`let (fn_id, args) = (*fn_id, args.clone());` at ~3122) although both borrows are immutable.
why: contains_trig_of_var runs during solve dispatch classification; the pattern is also a template others copy (the 'reduction covers the named case, misses the sibling' recurring shape applies to helpers too).
fix: Replace both sites with cas_solver_core::isolation_utils::contains_var and drop the args.clone() (iterate `args.iter()` under the immutable borrow). Mechanical, huella-safe.

title: Monomial factor ordering keyed by rendered display String (the seeded string-format-sort pattern), with structural compare_expr available in the same file
evidence: split_coeff_and_monomial (derive/collect.rs:237-250) builds `literal_factors.push((render_expr(ctx, factor), factor))` — a full formatter render per factor — then `literal_factors.sort_by(|l, r| l.0.cmp(&r.0))`. compare_expr is already imported and used 18 lines above (line 219). Related Debug/to_string hashing: eval_output_stats_hash/leaf.rs:5-6 hashes BigRational numer/denom via `.to_string()` and line 16 hashes Constants via `format!("{:?}", c)` — String allocation per leaf just to feed a Hasher.
why: Same core anti-pattern as the known cas_ast ConditionSet seed: display strings as ordering/hash keys are slow and make ordering depend on the formatter. collect.rs runs per target term in the combine-like-terms derive stage; the stats hash runs per eval output.
fix: collect.rs: sort by `compare_expr(ctx, *l, *r)` — verify with the didactic snapshot tests that focus-label rendering (which uses the same Vec) is unchanged, since the render string is also returned as a label. leaf.rs: hash numer/denom limbs (e.g. to_bytes_le) and the Constant discriminant (`std::mem::discriminant` is not stable-hashable, so an explicit match->u8 table) instead of formatted Strings.

title: Presentation-layer condition checks clone the full Context per condition and match conditions by rendered display strings
evidence: required_condition_is_impossible (eval_output_presentation_conditions.rs:1079-1098) does `let mut scratch = ctx.clone();` separately for Positive (1082) and NonNegative (1090) — one full arena clone PER CONDITION checked. Same pattern at wire/envelope/transparency/conditions.rs:16 and blocked_hint_format/filter.rs:142 (once per call). eval_output_condition_filter.rs:31/39 tests membership via `self.displays.contains(&cond.display(ctx))` — renders every condition to a String and linearly scans a Vec<String>.
why: Runs once per eval output (cold-ish), but the per-condition clone scales with session arena size, so long REPL/wire sessions pay increasingly for every emitted condition; it is also the pattern newcomers copy.
fix: Hoist one scratch clone above the condition loop in the caller and pass `&mut Context` into required_condition_is_impossible (interning is append-only, reuse is safe). For the filter, compare by normalized ExprId (or at least a HashSet<String>) instead of Vec<String> linear scans.

title: Commutative equality recursion tries both orderings per Add/Mul node with no (a,b) pair memo — exponential worst case on reordered wide sums
evidence: check_semantic_equality (semantic_equality.rs:193-205): `(Expr::Add(l1,r1), Expr::Add(l2,r2)) => (are_equal(l1,l2) && are_equal(r1,r2)) || (are_equal(l1,r2) && are_equal(r1,l2))` and the same for Mul — 4 recursive comparisons per commutative node pair, no memoization of visited (a,b) pairs. The `a == b` interning fast path (68-72) only helps when subtrees are IDENTICALLY interned; two n-term sums in different orders recurse with backtracking. Reached via strong_target_match/factors_match on every derive candidate and via engine cycle checks.
why: Latent super-linear blowup on wide reordered expressions — exactly the shape the derive multiset matcher feeds it (it falls back to are_equal_for_cycle_check when multiset matching fails). Would confirm with a micro-bench comparing two 20-term sums in reverse order.
fix: Thread a `HashSet<(ExprId, ExprId)>` visited/failed memo through the recursion (checker is a struct; add a RefCell field), or bound commutative backtracking depth and fall back to normalize-then-compare.

title: Crate-wide `ctx.get(id).clone()` in match position eagerly deep-clones BigRational/Vec payloads when most arms need only Copy ExprIds
evidence: 51 non-test `.get(..).clone()` sites in cas_solver (15 `match ctx.get(expr).clone()` in solve_backend_local.rs alone: 846, 899, 1170, 2105, 2296, 2427, 3018, 3045, 3855, 3886, 3918, 3940, 3957, 4644, 5947; plus recursive tree-walkers cloning per node: match_support.rs:139 canonicalize_inverse_trig_aliases, 330 extract_global_sign, 346, 376 project_root_like_calls_to_powers). Expr::clone allocates for Number(BigRational) (two BigInt heap clones) and Function/Matrix (Vec clone) — expression.rs:100-129 — even when the taken arm only destructures Copy ExprIds (Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Pow/Neg).
why: Individually small, but the recursive walkers pay it per node per call, and the pattern is the crate's default idiom, so every new handler inherits it. Alloc churn on solve dispatch and derive matching paths.
fix: Mechanical per-site: `match ctx.get(expr) { &Expr::Add(l, r) => ..., Expr::Function(f, args) => { let (f, args) = (*f, args.clone()); ... } }` — clone only in arms that outlive the borrow. Start with the recursive walkers (match_support.rs:139, 330, 376) where the per-node multiplier is highest.

title: Summary of lower-risk panic/unsafe/cast sites audited and judged guarded or unreachable from user input
evidence: Non-test panic-capable sites in cas_math are few and I inspected the risky ones: (1) unreachable!() in symbolic_integration_support.rs:2975 (only Tan/Cot reach the match), pattern_scanner.rs:458 (i in 0..3), complex_support.rs:185 (remainder mod 4 in 0..3), symbolic_integration_hyperbolic_reciprocal_policy.rs:291/325/343, limits_support.rs:1806, difference_of_squares_support.rs:178, trig_tan_triple_support.rs:187 — all are match-exhaustiveness guards protected by a local invariant. (2) verification_normalization.rs:224/381/562 `.expect("changed child normalization")` are guarded by the enclosing `if left != original_left || right != original_right`, so the Option is Some. (3) model.rs:487/495 `.expect(...includes derivative)` are builder invariants on internally-set status; no user path to a Verified/Failed status with derivative=None was found. (4) Casts: const_sign.rs:517 to_u32().unwrap_or(0), complex_support.rs:175 to_i32().unwrap_or(0), rationalize_diff_squares_support.rs:326 `n as i32` (n<=8 guard) are all bounded/defaulted. (5) No unsafe blocks, no std::fs/Command in cas_math library code. Web servers (web/server.py, server_colab.py): subprocess.run uses a list argv with input via stdin (no shell=True → no command injection), has per-request timeouts, session paths are sha256-hashed (no traversal), static serving via SimpleHTTPRequestHandler; the only notes are bind to all interfaces (''+PORT) plus CORS `*` (design choice for a local/Colab tool).
why: Documents what was checked so the universality campaign can treat these as verified-clean rather than re-auditing, and flags the one residual design note (bind-all + CORS*) that only matters if the web tool is exposed on an untrusted network alongside the power-hang DoS above.
fix: No change needed for the guarded panics; if the web server is ever exposed beyond localhost, bind to 127.0.0.1 by default and tighten CORS. Otherwise leave as-is.

title: Wildcard CORS plus all-interfaces bind expose the CAS subprocess to any origin/host
evidence: Both servers send `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on every response (server.py:962, 969, 977; server_colab.py:231, 436, 445, 455). server.py binds all interfaces via `HTTPServer(('', PORT), ...)` (server.py:994, empty host = 0.0.0.0) and server_colab.py defaults `DEFAULT_HOST="0.0.0.0"` (server_colab.py:40, 474). /api/eval spawns `cas_cli` with the user string as stdin (server.py:916-922).
why: With `*` CORS, any web page a victim visits can script requests to a locally/LAN-reachable ExpliCAS instance; combined with 0.0.0.0 binding this means the eval subprocess is invokable from other hosts on the network. Impact is bounded (the CLI is sandboxed to eval, session files under a temp dir), so P3 — but it is gratuitous exposure for a tool intended for localhost use.
fix: Default the bind host to 127.0.0.1 (require an explicit opt-in flag for 0.0.0.0), and either drop the wildcard CORS header or restrict it to the served origin; keep `*` only behind an explicit "public" mode.

title: Summary: lower-severity robustness observations that were checked and cleared or are minor
evidence: (1) rule_application.rs:215-243 also appends to a fixed `/tmp/rule_trace.log`, but it is gated behind the `CAS_TRACE_RULES` env var (mod.rs:38) so it is opt-in — lower risk than the depth-overflow write. (2) No `[profile.release] overflow-checks` in Cargo.toml, so i64 arithmetic wraps silently in release; the factor-collector `mult * k` sites (fraction_factors.rs:106, poly_gcd_structural.rs:228, integration_prep_support.rs:62-69) could in principle wrap, but I could NOT reach an overflow: nested powers are folded with BigInt before those collectors run (VERIFIED `((x^2000000000)^2000000000)^2000000000` → exact `x^8e27`, no wrap). (3) The `unreachable!()` sites sampled (define_rule! macro macros.rs, latex_parser.rs:373, parser.rs:453/655/728, arithmetic.rs trig-expansion) are all guarded by an enclosing match on the same discriminant that was just peeked/advanced, so they are genuinely unreachable from user input. (4) `.expect(&Token::...)` in latex_parser.rs are a Result-returning method, not Option::expect — no panic.
why: Recorded so the gaps are visible: the opt-in trace log and the release overflow-checks posture are worth a bounded hardening pass (turn on overflow-checks for release, or switch the collector accumulators to checked_mul returning None), but neither is a confirmed live crash/soundness bug today.
fix: Optionally set `overflow-checks = true` on `[profile.release]` (or use `checked_mul` in the i64 factor collectors), and gate/remove the trace log the same way as the depth-overflow write.

title: Wildcard CORS + bind-all-interfaces on an endpoint that spawns subprocesses (no auth/rate limit)
evidence: send_json (line 962) and send_json_error (line 969) set `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`; main() binds `HTTPServer(('', PORT), ...)` (line 994) — '' = 0.0.0.0, all interfaces. server_colab.py binds 0.0.0.0 (line 499 default) and uses ThreadingHTTPServer (line 461) with no concurrency cap, each /api/eval spawning a cas_cli subprocess (subprocess.run, list form — no shell injection). No authentication or rate limiting anywhere.
why: Any web page the user visits can POST to http://localhost:PORT/api/eval (CORS *) and drive local computation / read stored session results; on a LAN the bound-all server lets any host spawn unbounded concurrent subprocesses (fork-bomb DoS on the colab variant). Low severity for a dev tool but a real exposure surface.
fix: Default the bind address to 127.0.0.1, restrict Access-Control-Allow-Origin to the served origin, and cap concurrent evals (a bounded worker pool / semaphore) in the threading server.

title: i64 overflow in perfect-square detection: `m*m != k` can wrap in release / panic in debug
evidence: Lines 30-32: `let sqrt_k=(k as f64).sqrt(); let m=sqrt_k.round() as i64; if m*m != k || m==1 {...}` with k up to i64::MAX (split_numeric_factor returns k via `i64::try_from(n.to_integer())`, guarded only by `k<=0`). For k=i64::MAX, round(sqrt)=3037000500 and m*m=9223372037000250000 > i64::MAX, so `m*m` overflows — panics under [profile.dev]/[profile.test] (overflow-checks still on; Cargo.toml only sets debug=0) and wraps silently in release (overflow-checks off), where a wrapped m*m coincidentally equal to k would accept a bogus perfect square (soundness). Same shape at line 100-101 for the gcd path.
why: A release-mode wrap turns a guard meant to reject false factorizations into a potential wrong simplification of a discriminant surd; a debug/CI run could crash instead. Both are latent for the surd-heavy inputs the campaign targets.
fix: Compute the check with i128 or checked_mul: `m.checked_mul(m) != Some(k)`, or compare via BigInt before casting.

title: Residual low-severity items (dead code, saturating f64 cast, opt-in trace write)
evidence: Summary of verified-but-minor items: (1) web/server_colab.py:102-105 `_coerce_branch_mode` has a dead second `return value` after an unreachable one. (2) cas_solver_core/src/verification_runtime_flow.rs:417,428 `value as i64` on f64 relies on Rust's saturating float-cast for huge probe values — cosmetic (probe/counterexample display only), no panic. (3) cas_engine/.../rule_application.rs:215 appends to /tmp/rule_trace.log but is gated behind CAS_TRACE_RULES_ENABLED (opt-in, lower risk than the depth-overflow write). (4) POSITIVE: the in-scope panic audit is clean — see coverage.
why: None individually block the campaign; grouped so the signal-to-noise of the P1/P2 list stays high.
fix: Delete the dead return; leave the saturating cast; gate/relocate the trace write alongside the depth-overflow fix if that file is touched.

title: Summary of lower-severity / verified-safe items and residual exposure notes
evidence: (a) Web exposure: both servers set Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * on every response (server.py:962/969/976, server_colab.py:231/436/455) and bind all interfaces (server.py HTTPServer(('', PORT)) at 994; server_colab default host 0.0.0.0 at 40/474) with no auth while running cas_cli as a subprocess on arbitrary user input; subprocess timeouts exist (server.py:890-922, server_colab:379-391) so no unbounded hang there, but finding #2 still ties up a worker for the full timeout. (b) Silent numeric corruption: parser.rs:203/218/222 parse BigInt with .unwrap_or_else(|_| BigInt::from(0)) — a parse failure silently yields 0 rather than an error (currently unreachable because the digit regex pre-validates). (c) Verified SAFE (no action): cas_formatter/src/latex_core.rs:863/2453 relational_builtin_op_latex(name).unwrap() are guarded by a preceding .is_some() in the same match arm; cas_didactic build_mul_expr_from_factors expect (21364) is guarded by a match arm ensuring len>=2; the LazyLock Regex::new(...).unwrap() calls (latex_core.rs:16-21) are on constant patterns. (d) The large unwrap/expect counts in cas_session (175), cas_session_core, cas_ast/views.rs, cas_api_models/wire_types.rs are all inside #[cfg(test)] modules (e.g. cas_ast/src/views.rs panic!s at 1133+ are in tests; additive.rs panics at 337-509 are under #[cfg(test)] at line 325).
why: Documents the residual attack surface and records which flagged patterns were checked and dismissed, so the next audit does not re-litigate them.
fix: Optional: restrict web bind address / add a note that these servers are dev-only; make parser.rs BigInt parse failures return ParseError instead of silently 0.

title: Single-threaded HTTPServer serializes all requests so one slow expression stalls every client
evidence: server.py uses `http.server.HTTPServer` (synchronous, single-threaded) at L994, whereas server_colab.py deliberately uses `ThreadingHTTPServer` (L461). Each /api/eval blocks in subprocess.run up to the hard timeout (`_compute_subprocess_timeout_seconds`, up to 60-120s legacy cap). With a single request thread, one adversarial or genuinely heavy input blocks all other tabs/users until it completes or times out.
why: A trivial availability issue: a single `x^(10^6)`-class input freezes the whole REPL for other sessions for the entire timeout window. Low blast radius for a dev tool but a real robustness gap given multi-session support is a headline feature.
fix: Switch server.py to ThreadingHTTPServer (as server_colab already does); the per-session fcntl file lock already serializes snapshot access, so concurrent distinct sessions are safe.

title: i64 exponent silently truncated to i32 in base_pow_integer_rational (`m.unsigned_abs() as i32`)
evidence: solve_exponential_terms.rs:147 computes `let mag = b.pow(m.unsigned_abs() as i32);` where `m: i64` is an affine-exponent intercept/slope produced by affine_integer_exponent (line 112, i64). unsigned_abs() yields u64; `as i32` keeps only the low 32 bits and reinterprets the sign: m = 2^32 (4294967296) → 0 (so base^m collapses to 1); m in (2^31, 2^32) e.g. 4294967295 → -1 (Ratio::pow(-1) = reciprocal). Both are wrong values for base^m. Callers: exp_laurent_leaf (line 71) and exponential_base_and_coeff (lines 325-326), feeding the exponential equation handlers. I could NOT produce a user-visible wrong answer: probes like `solve(2^(x+4294967296) - 5*2^x + 6 = 0, x)` are intercepted by a symbolic factoring path that keeps 2^4294967296 exact, so the numeric collector isn't reached for those magnitudes.
why: Latent unsound truncation in a helper that the exponential solver family (being widened in the universality campaign) depends on; if any future handler routes a >=2^31 affine coefficient through this helper it will emit a silently wrong coefficient rather than declining.
fix: Take the exponent as u64/BigInt and reject (return None) when it exceeds a sane bound, or use a checked conversion (`i32::try_from(m.unsigned_abs()).ok()?`) so an out-of-range exponent declines instead of truncating. To CONFIRM reachability, add a unit test calling base_pow_integer_rational directly with m = 4294967296 and assert it does not return Some(1).

title: Web servers bind to all interfaces (0.0.0.0) with wildcard CORS, exposing the subprocess-backed engine to the LAN
evidence: server.py:994 `http.server.HTTPServer(('', PORT), CASHandler)` binds to '' (= 0.0.0.0, all interfaces), and every response sets `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` (send_json line 962, send_json_error line 969, do_OPTIONS line 976). server_colab.py:40 defaults DEFAULT_HOST='0.0.0.0'. The engine is invoked via subprocess.run with a list argv and `input=expression` over stdin (server.py:916-922; server_colab.py:384-391), so there is no shell/command injection, and static serving uses SimpleHTTPRequestHandler whose translate_path normalizes '..' (no path traversal). The residual risk is network exposure: any host on the LAN can POST /api/eval and drive CPU-bound symbolic computation (mitigated only by the per-request subprocess timeout).
why: For a SECURITY-dimension review this is the main externally-reachable surface; binding to localhost by default would remove unintended LAN exposure without affecting the intended local-dev workflow.
fix: Default the bind host to 127.0.0.1 (opt into 0.0.0.0 explicitly for Colab/tunnel use), and scope Access-Control-Allow-Origin to the served origin instead of '*'.

title: Sampled panic/indexing sites in cas_solver are guarded; no reachable user-input panic found (summary)
evidence: Counted risky patterns in non-test code: solve_backend_local.rs unwraps at 986/1975/2878/3000/4902/7271 are all `.pop()/.next().unwrap()` immediately inside `match len { 1 => ... }` or `if len==1` guards; the expect at 856 and derive_command.rs:904/950 are `.expect()` on an Option set to Some() in the preceding `if is_none()` block. The 11 unreachable! in derive/trig.rs (727,758,2133,2179,3072,3540,3691,7342,7369,7832,7852) and 9 in derive/hyperbolic.rs are terminal match arms reached only after the caller filters the function kind via `triple_angle_kind_for_fn(fn)?` / matches!(BuiltinFn::Sin|Cos) guards. Power-reduction arithmetic uses checked ops (trig.rs:3058-3060 `i64::checked_shl`, `binomial_i64(...)?`, `.checked_div(2)?`). No `unsafe` blocks exist anywhere in cas_solver/src. No runtime filesystem writes exist in cas_solver library code (the DEPTH_OVERFLOW_LOG_PATH append seed is in cas_engine/src/engine/transform/mod.rs:240, out of this crate's scope; it does write /tmp/cas_depth_overflow_expressions.log on depth overflow). Live robustness probes did not panic: `solve(x^^2=1,x)` -> clean 'Parse error', 400-deep `sin(...)` nesting hits the depth guard (returns unsimplified, no stack overflow), 2000-deep `((x+1)+1...)=0` returns {-2000}. Recursive solver descents are bounded (rational_function_of caps depth at 48, solve_backend_local.rs:1165).
why: Documents the panic/unsafe/overflow surface actually examined so the remaining gaps are visible; confirms the crate has no obvious reachable-panic or memory-safety defects beyond the specific findings above.
fix: No action for the guarded sites. If desired for defense-in-depth, the unreachable! arms could return None/Err instead of panicking, but none are currently reachable from user input.
